content: div id  attachment_587114  style  width  780px  class  wp-caption aligncenter   img loading  lazy  decoding  async  aria-describedby  caption-attachment-587114  class  wp-image-587114 size-full  src    alt  image of hands and drones on a table with SDI logo. The Sonoran Desert Institute is offering the ASUT educational program.  width  770  height  500  srcset    770w,   300w,   150w,   768w,   368w  sizes  auto,  max-width  770px  100vw, 770px     p id  caption-attachment-587114  class  wp-caption-text  The ASUT program at Sonoran Desert Institute offers education for those passionate about the rapidly growing UAS field.   Credit  SDI  p   div   div id  model-response-message-contentr_b281e77ad0d951a9  class  markdown markdown-main-panel tutor-markdown-rendering stronger enable-updated-hr-color preserve-whitespaces-in-response  dir  ltr  aria-live  polite  aria-busy  false    p data-path-to-node  0  As part of an ongoing STEM education series, this week we look at opportunities in the drone space. The Sonoran Desert Institute s Associate of Science in Uncrewed Technology, or ASUT, program offers a 100  distance-learning curriculum where students build their own drones and master technical flight skills to prepare for immediate hire in the expanding uncrewed aircraft systems industry.  p   p data-path-to-node  0  From soldering components to preparing for  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  FAA Part 107  a  certification, the program provides a comprehensive path for students to enter high-demand roles in  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  drone  a  maintenance, infrastructure inspection, and public safety.  p   p data-path-to-node  0  By blending 100  distance learning with a hands-on  build and fly  curriculum,  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  SDI  a  s ASUT program addresses a surging demand for skilled technicians in fields ranging from precision  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  agriculture  a  to  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  public safety  a .  p   p data-path-to-node  0  For  24,400, the 60-credit degree bridges the gap between basic drone operation and advanced flight testing, equipping students with the technical proficiency and Part 107 certification required to navigate the evolving legal and operational landscape of the uncrewed aerial systems  UAS  industry.  p    div   h2 What skills is the ASUT program teaching?  h2   p The ASUT  a href    utm_medium web utm_id robotreport  target  _blank  rel  noopener  program  a  is a novel certification course that enables distance learning through hands-on experience with real drones. Students progress by learning the UAS operations workflow and demonstrating proficiency both in the simulator and in real life.  p   p Most importantly, the students gain stick time and learn how to plan and operate real missions, while understanding the legal requirements to assess the risks and fly compliant missions for legal operations throughout the U.S.  p   p Students can prepare for and pass their  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  Part 107 certification  a , preparing them for immediate hire into UAS operational jobs.  p   p Students also learn about the proposed  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  14 CFR Part 108 requirements  a , which are preparing the way for beyond visual line-of-sight   a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  BVLOS  a   operations in the near future.  p   p The SDI ASUT program structure includes a certificate program and an associate degree in UAS technology, with the certificate serving as an introductory course for drone operation basics. The associate degree program builds upon the certificate by covering more advanced topics like remote sensing and flight operations management.  p   p The curriculum for SDI s UAS-213 and UAS-231 courses focuses on drone design validation and flight testing, respectively. In UAS-213, students learn to validate their drones through manual and autonomous flight testing.  p   p UAS-231 builds on these skills by teaching them how to work with test conductors, create test cards, and conduct comprehensive flight testing campaigns covering various aspects like aerodynamics, sensors, and human factors.  p   p The program also covers both technical skills and professional development, with students creating video content for their portfolio and learning specific industry applications like thermography and GIS data collection. The GIS course includes practical training on client interactions, flight planning, and data analysis using tools like  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  DroneDeploy  a , culminating in the creation of professional reports.  p   p The current cost of the program is  24,400, with financial assistance available. SDI offers different scholarships that help military-connected and first-responder students pay for their education.  p   p Schools like Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University in Florida and Arizona offer a Bachelor of Science in Uncrewed Aircraft Systems with a  Design and Build  track. This is more engineering-heavy but excellent for technicians aiming for manufacturer-level roles.  p   hr     div style  text-align  center    a href     img loading  lazy  decoding  async  class  aligncenter size-full wp-image-568305  src    alt  SITE AD for the 2026 Robotics Summit save the date.  width  728  height  90      a   div   hr     h2 What job opportunities are available?  h2   p There are a number of emerging employment opportunities in the field of UAS operations.  p   h3 Maintenance and engineering roles  h3   p These roles focus on the  hardware  side keeping the fleet airworthy and integrating new sensors.  p   ul   li aria-level  1   b UAS maintenance technician  b   Primarily a mechanic for a drone fleet. This involves troubleshooting flight controllers, repairing carbon fiber frames, and replacing motors ESC units.  ul   li aria-level  2   em Key employers   em  Defense contractors such as  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  AeroVironment  a  and  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  General Atomics  a , as well as commercial fleet operators.  li    ul    li   li aria-level  1   b Avionics technician  b   Specialized in the electronic  brain  of the drone, including GPS modules, data links, and SATCOM systems.  li   li aria-level  1   b Production assembly technician  b   Working in manufacturing facilities to build and test new drone models before they are shipped to customers.  li    ul   h3 Specialized flight operations  h3   p These jobs require a mix of Part 107 piloting skills and technical knowledge to operate complex payloads.  p   ul   li aria-level  1   b Agricultural spray pilot  b   Operating large-scale heavy-lift drones to apply fertilizers or pesticides. This is a booming field due to its precision compared to traditional tractors.  li   li aria-level  1   b  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  Infrastructure  a   a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  inspector  a   b   Using thermal and lidar sensors to inspect power lines, cell towers, or bridges. Technicians are preferred here because they can perform minor  field repairs  if a drone malfunctions in a remote area.  li   li aria-level  1   b Flight test operator  b   Working for drone manufacturers to put prototype aircraft through  torture tests  to find failure points and validate software updates.  li    ul   h3 Data and systems integration  h3   p For graduates who focused on the software and sensor side of their training.  p   ul   li aria-level  1   b GIS  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  lidar  a  technician  b   Collecting and processing 3D maps and topographical data. These technicians aren t just flying  they are ensuring the high-end sensors  costing  20k   are calibrated and that the data is accurate.  li   li aria-level  1   b UAS solutions engineer  b   Helping companies such as  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  construction  a  or  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  mining  a  firms figure out which drone systems they need to buy and training their internal staff on how to use them.  li    ul   h3 Public safety and defense  h3   p The public sector is growing as UAS technology evolves, becomes more resilient, and is useful for emerging sectors.  p   ul   li aria-level  1   b Public safety UAS coordinator   b  Working for police or fire departments to manage their drone programs, maintain equipment, and ensure FAA compliance.  li   li  b Field service representative  FSR    b  Often involves travel  sometimes overseas  to support military UAS operations. These roles frequently require a security clearance, but they can offer the highest pay in the industry.  li    ul   p  em This article is a part of our ongoing  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  STEM education series  a , looking at education and training programs for robotics and STEM-related careers.  em   p   p The post  a href    offers ASUT drone operations certificate program  a  appeared first on  a href    Robot Report  a .  p
